title: poa pulchella
intro: Poa pulchella, also known as the blue fescue grass, is a small perennial grass native to New Zealand and southeastern Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Poa pulchella include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Poa pulchella can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, or used in topical ointments and creams to harness its medicinal properties.
